You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the mbed-os codebase.
ARM Mbed OS is an embedded operating system for ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers. It includes a hardware abstraction layer (HAL), RTOS primitives, peripheral drivers (SPI, I2C, UART, CAN, USB), connectivity (BLE, Wi-Fi, cellular), storage, and security. C/C++ codebase.

TOOL USAGE — you MUST use these exact formats:

To run a shell command:
<bash>your_command_here</bash>

To view a file:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>

To view specific lines:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor> [start_line, end_line]

To edit a file (replace text):
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>exact text to replace</old_str>
<new_str>replacement text</new_str>

When finished, say: SUBMIT

IMPORTANT: Each response must contain EXACTLY ONE tool call. Do NOT put multiple tool calls in one response.

Working directory is /repo.
Think step by step. First understand the code, then investigate, then fix.

There is a copy-paste error where duplicated code was not correctly updated for its new context downstream of function Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt in the targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6 subsystem.
The function is located at targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h:647.
Please investigate and fix this issue.

Let me first examine the function `Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt` and its surrounding context to understand the copy-paste error.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [640, 660]

* Sets one or more interrupt source statuses (sets an INTR_SET register).
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.
* \param interrupt
*  The interrupt bit mask \ref group_pdm_pcm_macros_interrupt_masks.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE void Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt(PDM_Type * base, uint32_t interrupt)
{
    CY_ASSERT_L2(CY_PDM_PCM_IS_INTR_MASK_VALID(interrupt));
    PDM_PCM_INTR_SET(base) = interrupt;
}


/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_GetNumInFifo
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Reports the current number of used words in the output data FIFO.
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.

Let me see more of the file to understand the context and find the potential copy-paste error downstream of this function.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [640, 700]

* Sets one or more interrupt source statuses (sets an INTR_SET register).
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.
* \param interrupt
*  The interrupt bit mask \ref group_pdm_pcm_macros_interrupt_masks.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE void Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt(PDM_Type * base, uint32_t interrupt)
{
    CY_ASSERT_L2(CY_PDM_PCM_IS_INTR_MASK_VALID(interrupt));
    PDM_PCM_INTR_SET(base) = interrupt;
}


/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_GetNumInFifo
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Reports the current number of used words in the output data FIFO.
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.
* \return The current number of used FIFO words (range is 0 - 254).
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE uint8_t Cy_PDM_PCM_GetNumInFifo(PDM_Type const * base)
{
    return (uint8_t) (_FLD2VAL(PDM_RX_FIFO_STATUS_USED, PDM_PCM_RX_FIFO_STATUS(base)));
}


/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearFifo
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Resets the output data FIFO, removing all data words from the FIFO.
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE void Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearFifo(PDM_Type * base)
{
    PDM_PCM_RX_FIFO_CTL(base) |= PDM_RX_FIFO_CTL_CLEAR_Msk; /* clear FIFO and disable it */
    PDM_PCM_RX_FIFO_CTL(base) &= (uint32_t) ~PDM_RX_FIFO_CTL_CLEAR_Msk; /* enable FIFO */
}


/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_ReadFifo
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Reads ("pops") one word from the output data FIFO.
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.
* \return The data word.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE uint32_t Cy_PDM_PCM_ReadFifo(PDM_Type const * base)
{
    return (PDM_PCM_RX_FIFO_RD(base));
}

Let me continue examining the file to find where the copy-paste error might be. I need to look further downstream to see if there are functions that were incorrectly copied from `Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt`.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [700, 800]

Let me check for any functions that might have been duplicated from `Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt` but not properly updated. Let me look for functions that might set interrupt masks or similar functionality.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [500, 650]

Now I can see the issue! Looking at the functions, I can see that `Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterruptMask` and `Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt` are very similar, but there's a copy-paste error in the documentation and implementation. 

Let me check if there are other similar functions that might have the same issue:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [800, 850]

Let me check if there are other functions that might have been incorrectly copied. Let me look for any functions that might set interrupt-related registers but have incorrect documentation or implementation:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [1, 50]

/***************************************************************************//**
* \file cy_pdm_pcm.h
* \version 2.30
*
* The header file of the PDM_PCM driver.
*
********************************************************************************
* \copyright
* Copyright 2016-2020 Cypress Semiconductor Corporation
* SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
*
* Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
* you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
* You may obtain a copy of the License at
*
*     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
*
* Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
* distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
* WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
* See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
* limitations under the License.
*******************************************************************************/

/**
* \addtogroup group_pdm_pcm
* \{
*
* The pulse-density modulation to pulse-code modulation (PDM-PCM) driver provides an
* API to manage PDM-PCM conversion. A PDM-PCM converter is used
* to convert 1-bit digital audio streaming data to PCM data.
*
* The functions and other declarations used in this driver are in cy_pdm_pcm.h.
* You can include cy_pdl.h to get access to all functions
* and declarations in the PDL.
*
* Features:
* * Supports FIFO buffer for Incoming Data
* * Supports Software Mute Mode
* * Programmable Gain Settings
* * Programmable Word Length
*
* Pulse-density modulation, or PDM, represents
* an analog signal with a binary signal. In a PDM signal, specific amplitude values
* are not encoded into codewords of pulses of different weight as they would be
* in pulse-code modulation (PCM); rather, the relative density of the pulses corresponds
* to the analog signal's amplitude. The output of a 1-bit DAC is the same
* as the PDM encoding of the signal.
*
* Pulse-code modulation (PCM) is the method used to digitally represent sampled analog signals.

Let me search for any other functions that might have similar copy-paste issues by looking for patterns in the interrupt-related functions:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [580, 640]

Now I can see the issue clearly. Looking at the functions, I can see that there's a copy-paste error in the `Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt` function. The documentation says it "Clears one or more PDM-PCM interrupt statuses (sets an INTR register's bits)" but it should say it clears by writing to the INTR register, not sets. Also, there might be an issue with the implementation.

Let me check if there's a dedicated interrupt clear register:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [1, 100]

/***************************************************************************//** * \file cy_pdm_pcm.h * \version 2.30 * * The header file of the PDM_PCM driver. * ******************************************************************************** * \copyright * Copyright 2016-2020 Cypress Semiconductor Corporation * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 * * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License. * You may obtain a copy of the License at * * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 * * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and * limitations under the License. *******************************************************************************/ /** * \addtogroup group_pdm_pcm * \{ * * The pulse-density modulation to pulse-code modulation (PDM-PCM) driver provides an * API to manage PDM-PCM conversion. A PDM-PCM converter is used * to convert 1-bit digital audio streaming data to PCM data. * * The functions and other declarations used in this driver are in cy_pdm_pcm.h. * You can include cy_pdl.h to get access to all functions * and declarations in the PDL. * * Features: * * Supports FIFO buffer for Incoming Data * * Supports Software Mute Mode * * Programmable Gain Settings * * Programmable Word Length * * Pulse-density modulation, or PDM, represents * an analog signal with a binary signal. In a PDM signal, specific amplitude values * are not encoded into codewords of pulses of different weight as they would be * in pulse-code modulation (PCM); rather, the relative density of the pulses corresponds * to the analog signal's amplitude. The output of a 1-bit DAC is the same * as the PDM encoding of the signal. * * Pulse-code modulation (PCM) is the method used to digitally represent sampled analog signals. * It is the standard form of digital audio in computers, compact discs, digital telephony, * and other digital audio applications. In a PCM stream, the amplitude of the analog signal * is sampled regularly at uniform intervals, and each sample is quantized * to the nearest value within a range of digital steps. * * \section group_pdm_pcm_configuration_considerations Configuration Considerations * * To set up a PDM-PCM, provide the configuration parameters in the * \ref cy_stc_pdm_pcm_config_t structure. * * For example, set dataStreamingEnable to true, configure rxFifoTriggerLevel, * dmaTriggerEnable (depending on whether DMA is going to be used), * provide clock settings (clkDiv, mclkDiv and ckoDiv), set sincDecRate * to the appropriate decimation rate, wordLen, and wordBitExtension. * No other parameters are necessary for this example. * * To initialize the PDM-PCM block, call the \ref Cy_PDM_PCM_Init function, providing the * filled \ref cy_stc_pdm_pcm_config_t structure. * * If you use a DMA, the DMA channel should be previously configured. PDM-PCM interrupts * (if applicable) can be enabled by calling \ref Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterruptMask. * * For example, if the trigger interrupt is used during operation, the ISR * should call the \ref Cy_PDM_PCM_ReadFifo as many times as required for your * FIFO payload. Then call \ref Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt with appropriate parameters. * * If a DMA is used and the DMA channel is properly configured, no CPU activity * (or application code) is needed for PDM-PCM operation. * * \section group_pdm_pcm_more_information More Information * See: the PDM-PCM chapter of the device technical reference manual (TRM); * the PDM_PCM_PDL Component datasheet; * CE219431 - PSOC 6 MCU PDM-TO-PCM EXAMPLE. * * \section group_pdm_pcm_changelog Changelog * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
VersionChangesReason for Change
2.30Fixed MISRA 2012 violations.MISRA 2012 compliance.
2.20.2Minor documentation updates.Documentation enhancement.
2.20.1Snippet updated.

Let me look for any macros or definitions that might give me clues about the correct register names:

<bash>grep -n "PDM_PCM_INTR" /repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</bash>

Now I can see the issue clearly. Looking at the interrupt-related functions, I can see that there's a copy-paste error in the documentation. Let me examine the functions more carefully:

1. `Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterruptMask` - Sets the interrupt mask register
2. `Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt` - Should clear interrupts by writing to the INTR register
3. `Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt` - Sets interrupt flags by writing to the INTR_SET register

The issue is in the documentation of `Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt`. The documentation says "Clears one or more PDM-PCM interrupt statuses (sets an INTR register's bits)" which is contradictory - you don't "set" bits to clear interrupts. The correct description should be something like "Clears one or more PDM-PCM interrupt statuses by writing to the INTR register."

Let me fix this copy-paste error:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Clears one or more PDM-PCM interrupt statuses (sets an INTR register's bits).
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address
* \param interrupt
*  The interrupt bit mask \ref group_pdm_pcm_macros_interrupt_masks.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE void Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt(PDM_Type * base, uint32_t interrupt)
{
    CY_ASSERT_L2(CY_PDM_PCM_IS_INTR_MASK_VALID(interrupt));
    PDM_PCM_INTR(base) = interrupt;
    (void) PDM_PCM_INTR(base);
}</old_str>
<new_str>/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Clears one or more PDM-PCM interrupt statuses by writing to the INTR register.
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address
* \param interrupt
*  The interrupt bit mask \ref group_pdm_pcm_macros_interrupt_masks.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE void Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt(PDM_Type * base, uint32_t interrupt)
{
    CY_ASSERT_L2(CY_PDM_PCM_IS_INTR_MASK_VALID(interrupt));
    PDM_PCM_INTR(base) = interrupt;
    (void) PDM_PCM_INTR(base);
}</new_str>
Replacement successful in /repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h. Found 1 occurrences
SUBMIT
Agent submitted.
